This might be one of the coolest Airbnbs I’ve ever seen — a whole train car turned into the most unique stay in Alabama!

Sleeper-car rooms, stunning views from the balcony, a full kitchen, huge shower, plus a pool, hot tub, cornhole, mini golf, and even s’mores. They're losing it over how special it is… and honestly, I can see why!

I was not expecting the inside to be so charming and large.

Have you ever stayed somewhere that felt completely unique and special? Or what’s the coolest unexpected place you’ve discovered?

kept.text:
Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky reveals his darkest moment amid COVID-19

"You're about to go public and working on one of the biggest IPOs ever. Then suddenly you lose 80% of your business in eight weeks"

"It's like an 18-wheeler going 80 miles an hour and slamming on the brakes. Nothing good comes out of that situation"

"Suddenly, the question was whether I was enough of a leader to be able to man through a crisis. And that crisis occurred on March 15th when the world shutdown"

"I held an emergency board meeting, and I remember a quote by Andy Grove, one of the founders of Intel, and he said, bad companies are destroyed by a crisis. Good companies survive a crisis, but great companies are defined by a crisis"

kept.text:
If you ever feel like giving up,

Starbucks faced 217 investor rejections… yet grew to 41,000+ stores across 87 countries.

Canva was rejected over 100 times with “graphic design is already solved.” Now earning $3.5B+ annually with 260M+ active users.

Airbnb was dismissed as a ridiculous idea… now a 85B dollar hospitality giant.

Shakira was told she couldn’t sing, she went on to win 4 Grammys and 300+ awards.

The Ordinary’s branding was called “too plain to sell”… yet it became a $2.2 billion beauty empire.

Dyson built over 5,000 prototypes before creating one of the world’s top vacuum brands.

Rejection isn’t the end, it’s often just the beginning.

kept.text:
> Silicon Valley didn’t create this billionaire !
>He couldn’t even pay his rent.
> Meet Brian Chesky.
> Born in 1981 in New York, USA.
>Not a coder. Studied industrial design.
>Moved to San Francisco with no clear plan.
> 2007. Rent was due. He had no money.
>Conference in town → hotels sold out.
>Put 3 air mattresses in his living room.
>Charged strangers to stay.
> Called it “Air Bed & Breakfast.”
> Teamed up with Joe Gebbia and later Nathan Blecharczyk.
> Investors said:
“Strangers won’t stay in other people’s homes.”
>Rejected again. And again. And again.
> Ran out of money.
>Sold custom cereal boxes — “Obama O’s” & “Cap’n McCain’s”, just to keep the company alive 💀
> Got into Y Combinator in 2009.
Finally got a shot.
> Focused on one thing: trust.
Real photos. Reviews. Better experience.
> Turned a crazy idea into a global platform: Airbnb
> 2010 → barely known
>2020 → pandemic hits travel hard
>Lost ~80% of bookings in weeks
> Didn’t quit.Cut costs. Refocused. Rebuilt.
> Took Airbnb public in 2020.
Valued at ~$47 billion on IPO day 

> Today:
150M+ users
220+ countries & regions

> From air mattresses → global travel giant.

Said: “Build something people love.”
> Broke designer → billionaire founder
 Absolute legend

quoted_tweet.text:
Gospa @AstaVrecko iz stranke Levica v svoji razpravi o interventnem zakonu pozablja, da ne živi na ideološkem otoku.

Airbnb, kot rada etiketira za demagoško diskriminacijo, so mali ponudniki zasebnih nastanitev, ki s svojim delom predstavljajo tisti pravi človeški obraz slovenskega turizma.

To niso kapitalisti. To so ambasadorji slovenskega turizma, ki s svojim delom polnijo številne družinske proračune, občinske proračune, državne proračune, iz katerih se napajajo tako očitno radodarno tudi ministrstva, kot je bilo njeno, poslanske plače, in nazadnje tudi javne kulturne prireditve v turističnih krajih.

Moratorij na Zakon o gostinstvu je nujen sistemski ukrep za preživetje vsega tega. Gre za ukrep, ki pomaga preprečevati rušilnost Hanovega zakona o gostinstvu, ki so ga pomagali pisati tudi v ideološkem laboratoriju Levice na Ministrstvu za solidarno prihodnost, ker so se očitno raje kot z reševanjem stanovanjskih vprašanj ukvarjali z iskanjem dežurnih krivcev oziroma grešnih kozlov.

Kako plodno je bilo to njihovo delo, pa nenazadnje pričajo rezultati rekordnih rasti cen stanovanj v zadnjem četrtletju lanskega leta, torej neposredno po sprejemu samega Zakona o gostinstvu. Torej smo z ukrepom, ki so ga predstavljali za rešitev stanovanjske problematike, v Sloveniji dobili najvišjo rast cen stanovanj v celotni Evropski uniji. Pa razumi, kdor more, to logiko.

quoted_tweet.text:
Microsoft even disabled the ICC Chief prosecutor Karim Khan and his team's email accounts on behalf of Israel. Francesca Albanese was cut off from all her bank accounts; unable to open new ones or use/ get credit cards. 

When you confront them, they will come at you for everything, from every angle— to discredit and silence you. They control the banking systems through which money flows globally, whether through financial institutions or intermediaries like Stripe.

When the US sanctioned Albanese last year, it wasn't just her money that was frozen— her hotel bookings were cancelled, her health insurance refused to reimburse her, she couldn't even use most apps. Even the Christmas gifts her 13-year-old daughter received were sanctioned.

And she was reprimanded only after she began focusing her attention on the powerful American corporations complicit in funding and enabling the genocide in Gaza. 

She wrote letters to 48 companies, universities and financial institutions notifying them that they were complicit in and profiting from a genocide— the Trump Administration instantly moved to punish her.

They have infiltrated and can manipulate every facet of your existence in ways beyond your wildest imagination. They will try to exploit every component of your life in order to make it work against you.

Remain cognizant of this in every aspect of what you think is your *private* life. They don't just control the weapons that kill, but the algorithms on your phone that can be used to dictate your life as well.

民泊2軒目もたまたま事業譲渡でした。

赤字で営業中の物件。
お部屋やリスティングを見ると、清掃がかなり甘かったり、1週間前には予約ブロック、価格調整してる形跡もないなど、代行業者の運営から私の自主運営に切り替えたら黒字化できると思いました

返還される敷金分を公庫で融資(2度目)を引き、物品譲渡代金含む残りは自己資金。
初期費用は利益ベースで11ヵ月で回収見込みで、8ヵ月が経ちました。

売上は引渡し前に比べると2倍ほどになり、2軒目もゲストチョイスキープ。
(1件だけ、「また来たいです」の⭐️4で悔しい🥲) 

こちらはオンラインサロンminkaで共有された物件情報の中から見つけました。
70㎡3LDKの「中箱」タイプ、墨田区の駅徒歩8分。
中庭付き、リフォームしたての素敵なお部屋です。

でも家賃が高い。民泊物件の取得競争が加熱する中でここならライバルが少ないのでは？と考え内見、契約に至りました
